title: Digital Signature Algorithm (DSA)
text: Digital Signature Algorithm (DSA) The Digital Signature Algorithm (DSA) is a FIPS-approved asymmetric cryptographic method designed solely for generating and validating digital signatures. It provides authentication, data integrity, and non-repudiation, but does not support encryption or key exchange. In this process, the sender signs the message with a private key, and the receiver verifies it with the public key. Table 1. Supported DSA Algorithms Algorithm Meaning DSA_SHA1 Digital Signature Algorithm with SHA-1 hash function DSA_SHA224 Digital Signature Algorithm with SHA-224 hash function DSA_SHA256 Digital Signature Algorithm with SHA-256 hash function DSA_SHA384 Digital Signature Algorithm with SHA-384 hash function DSA_SHA512 Digital Signature Algorithm with SHA-512 hash function Generating Keys Uniface accepts the PKCS #8 private key format and X.509 public key format. For interoperability, PEM (Privacy Enhanced Mail) encoding is used for both keys. PEM encoding is a Base64 encoding with a header and footer. This encoding is commonly used. You can generate the RSA private and public keys using OpenSSL. For example, the following command generates a 2048 -bit key saved to file named myPrivateKey.pem : # Generate the RSA private key in the traditional SSLeay format. openssl genrsa -out traditional.pem 2048 # Change the private key to PKCS#8 format in PEM encoding. openssl pkcs8 -topk8 -in traditional.pem -inform pem -nocrypt -out myPrivateKey.pem -outform pem Copy To generate the corresponding public key: openssl rsa -in myPrivateKey.pem -out myPublicKey.pem -pubout Copy For more information, consult the OpenSSL documentation ( http://www.openssl.org/ ). Example: Signing Messages Sign a message by DSA with SHA-256 hash: variables String vMessage, vMyPrivateKey Raw vSignature endvariables vSignature = "" vMessage = "This is secret data." vSignature = $encode("DSA_SHA256", vMessage, vMyPrivateKey) Copy Example: Verifying Messages Verify the signed message: variables String vMessage, vMyPublicKey Raw vSignature endvariables if ($decode("DSA_SHA256", vMessage, vMyPublicKey, vSignature)) message/info "This is a valid message." else message/info "Signature doesn’t match with the message." endif Copy Was this topic helpful?
